body: Budiyanto Djoko Susanto, a commissioner at PT Sumber Alfaria Trijaya Tbk (AMRT), earned an estimated IDR 90.27 billion from divesting 31.67 million shares at IDR 2,850 per share, leaving him with a 0.25% stake in the company. The transaction has caused AMRT's shares to fluctuate, closing at IDR 2,850 per share, a 1.38% correction from the beginning of the year, with PT Sigmantara Alfa holding the largest stake at 53.19%.
title: Budiyanto Djoko Susanto earns estimated IDR 90.27 billion from divestment of 31.67 million shares in PT Sumber Alfaria Trijaya.

body: Ikin Wirawan acquired 10.36% of PT Wira Global Solusi Tbk (WGSH) shares, purchasing 108.02 million shares, while PT Silicon Valley Connection sold 78.56 million shares, reducing its stake from 15.27% to 7.73%. Meanwhile, other investors made notable transactions, including CGS International Securities Singapore Pte Ltd increasing its stake in PT Astrindo Nusantara Infrastruktur Tbk (ASTR) by 85 million shares, and Newton Capital Ltd releasing 1.10 billion shares of PT MDTV Media Technologies Tbk (MDTV), reducing its stake from 10.67% to 8%.
title: Ikin Wirawan acquires 10.36% stake in WGS Ventures, multiple investors make significant transactions in various Indonesian companies.

body: PT Cardig Aero Service Tbk (CASS) has reached an agreement with PT Cardig Asset Management (CAM) to restructure its debt, resulting in a 20% reduction in loan interest worth IDR 27.29 billion (USD 317,143) and a rescheduling of loan interest payments over 6 installments from January to June 2025. The debt restructuring agreement will reduce CASS's total liabilities by IDR 136.49 billion (USD 1.58 million), equivalent to 2.11% of its total equity as of November 2024.
title: PT Cardig Aero Service reaches debt restructuring agreement with PT Cardig Asset Management.

body: PT Ace Oldfields Tbk (KUAS) will distribute its first-ever interim dividend of IDR 1.94 billion, or IDR 1.5 per share, with a payout ratio of 52%, marking the first time the company has distributed an interim dividend since its IPO in 2021. The company's net profit attributable to the parent entity saw a 2.41% year-on-year increase to IDR 3.73 billion in September 2024, with retained earnings of IDR 23.38 billion and total equity of IDR 156.95 billion.
title: PT Ace Oldfields to Distribute IDR 1.94 Billion Interim Dividend for 2024 Fiscal Year

body: The Indonesia Stock Exchange (IDX) has seen 41 companies debut through initial public offerings (IPOs) as of December 20, 2024, falling short of the target of 62 companies, but with 22 potential listings still in the pipeline. The pipeline includes 19 companies with large assets, 1 with assets below IDR 50 billion, and 2 mid-range companies, with 8 potential issuers scheduled to debut in January 2025.
title: Number of companies going public on Indonesia Stock Exchange falls short of target at 62.

body: PT Sarana Menara Nusantara Tbk.'s five subsidiaries have secured a loan worth IDR 2 trillion from PT Bank Permata Tbk., with the loan split into two revolving facilities worth IDR 1 trillion each, to promote growth and contribute positively to the group. The loan, which will not negatively impact the company's operational financial activities, will be used to boost the subsidiaries' capital, with each subsidiary granted a specific loan amount, including PT Profesional Telekomunikasi, PT Iforte Solusi, PT Solusi Tunas Pratama, Bit Teknologi, and PT Iforte Payment Infrastructure.
title: PT Sarana Menara Nusantara's Five Subsidiaries Secure IDR 2 Trillion Loan from PT Bank Permata.

body: PT Communication Cable Systems Indonesia Tbk (CCSI) has established two new subsidiaries, PT Lucky Joint Indonesia with IDR 48 billion authorized capital and IDR 12 billion paid-up capital, and PT CCSI Niagatama Digital with IDR 5 billion authorized capital and IDR 1.25 billion paid-up capital, to support its business activities in telecommunication cable, internet services, and telecommunication network construction. The new subsidiaries will not have a material impact on CCSI's operational, legal, financial activities, or business sustainability, according to Director Corporate Secretary Irawan Mario N. Palilingan.
title: PT Communication Cable Systems Indonesia establishes two new subsidiaries to expand its business activities in the telecommunication industry.

body: The Jakarta Stock Price Composite Index rose 0.75% or 52.71 points to 7,036.57, driven by gains in blue-chip state-owned stocks, including PT Telkom Indonesia Tbk (TLKM) which jumped 7.48% and contributed 22.42 index points. The stock exchange saw a transaction value of IDR 31.92 trillion (USD 1.97 billion) and a net buy of IDR 128.78 billion from foreign investors, a reversal from the previous week's net sell of IDR 4.09 trillion.
title: Jakarta Stock Price Composite Index Rises 0.75% Led by Gains in State-Owned Blue-Chip Stocks

body: PT Startel Communication (SC) has increased its stake in PT Jaya Trishindo Tbk (HELI) to 27.15% by acquiring 2.4 million shares, up from 26.86% previously, making it one of the controlling shareholders of the air transportation logistics service company. The transaction has resulted in a 2.18% increase in Heli's share price to IDR 274 per share, with SC now owning 226.13 million shares, followed by other major shareholders including PT Komala Tri Varia, UOB Kay Hian Pte Ltd, and PT Avia Nusantara.
title: PT Startel Communication increases stake in PT Jaya Trishindo to 27.15% with 2.4 million share purchase.

body: PT Sarana Menara Nusantara Tbk (TOWR) is postponing its capital increase through pre-emptive rights, citing the current macroeconomic situation and stock price trend, and will reevaluate the rights issue structure to fit the current market trend. The company had planned to issue 4.99 billion new shares at IDR 900 per share, aiming to raise IDR 4.49 trillion in fresh funds, but its stock price has slipped 37.13% or 375 points since the beginning of the year.
title: PT Sarana Menara Nusantara Tbk postpones rights issue due to current macroeconomic situation.

body: PT Petrindo Jaya Kreasi TBK (CUAN) has started producing metallurgical coal through its subsidiary, PT Daya Bumindo Karunia, with an integrated production facility and supporting infrastructure, backed by a IDR 2.43 trillion credit facility from PT Bank Negara TBK. The company expects the partnership with PT Petrosea TBK to contribute positively to its bottom line, promoting production and transportation efficiency.
title: PT Petrindo Jaya Kreasi TBK expands business portfolio with metallurgical coal production through subsidiary PT Daya Bumindo Karunia.

body: Foreign investors sold a net 15.98 million shares on the Indonesian stock exchange, with PT Goto Gojek Tokopedia Tbk (GOTO) experiencing the highest net sell of 447.61 million shares. Meanwhile, PT Bumi Resources Minerals Tbk (BRMS) saw the largest net buy of 108.77 million shares, followed by PT Maharaksa Biru Energi Tbk with a net buy of 100.36 million shares.
title: Foreign Investors Sell 15.98 Million Shares in Indonesian Stock Exchange on Friday
